description: Narrow exceptions for legacy machine contracts and immutable upstream provenance. Entries are ordered from specific to broad; expectedOccurrences locks each exception against growth.

entries.reason: The audit policy must spell the legacy identities it classifies.
entries.removeWhen: Remove each token only after its final compatibility or provenance entry is retired.

entries.reason: The scanner must recognize every frozen legacy identity.
entries.removeWhen: Remove a token after its compatibility and provenance contracts no longer exist.

entries.reason: The upstream package map and repository link are immutable fork lineage.
entries.removeWhen: Never while this repository derives from the recorded upstream baseline.

entries.reason: Exact upstream-to-downstream mappings are required for artifact normalization and attribution.
entries.removeWhen: Never while the fork publishes artifacts derived from this upstream baseline.

entries.reason: Published upstream changelog entries are historical release evidence.
entries.removeWhen: Never; append downstream release notes instead of rewriting released history.

entries.reason: Captured goal audit logs, patches, and reports must remain verbatim.
entries.removeWhen: Never; these files are immutable audit evidence.

entries.reason: The captured evidence paths retain the upstream directory seam.
entries.removeWhen: Only during a separately reviewed repository path migration.

entries.reason: The remediation record documents upstream behavior and commands.
entries.removeWhen: Never; supersede it with a new report.

entries.reason: The deep-audit output is historical evidence rather than current product copy.
entries.removeWhen: Never; generate a new dated audit instead.
